command_or_instruction: Download and install from https://nodejs.org/ (LTS version). On Windows check 'Add to PATH'.
expected_result: Run 'node --version' and see v18.x or higher
notes: Required for CDK and frontend build

command_or_instruction: Download and install from https://python.org/. On Windows check 'Add Python to PATH'.
notes: Required for Lambda layer build

command_or_instruction: Download and install from https://aws.amazon.com/cli/
notes: Required for all AWS operations

command_or_instruction: Run: aws configure -- Enter Access Key ID, Secret Access Key, region 'us-east-1', output 'json'
expected_result: Run 'aws sts get-caller-identity' and see your account ID
notes: IAM user needs AdministratorAccess and aws-marketplace:Subscribe permission

command_or_instruction: Run: npm install -g aws-cdk
notes: Deploys infrastructure to AWS

command_or_instruction: Go to https://console.aws.amazon.com/ and sign in. Set region to us-east-1 (N. Virginia) in top-right dropdown.
expected_result: Console shows N. Virginia as region
notes: All resources must be in us-east-1

command_or_instruction: Search 'Bedrock' in console search bar. Click Amazon Bedrock. Click 'Model catalog' in left sidebar.
expected_result: You see a list of available foundation models
notes: Models are enabled by default in commercial regions

action: Complete Anthropic First Time Use form
command_or_instruction: Click any Anthropic Claude model (e.g. Claude 3.5 Haiku). Fill out the form: company name, website, intended users (Internal), industry, use case ('Internal document search and generation'). Submit.
notes: One-time per account. If no form appears, access is already enabled. Amazon Titan models need no form.

command_or_instruction: Go to Bedrock > Playground. Select Claude 3.5 Haiku. Type 'Hello' and click Run.
notes: If error, check IAM has aws-marketplace:Subscribe permission

command_or_instruction: Go to https://whatismyip.com. Note your IPv4 address.
notes: This IP goes in the WAF allowlist

command_or_instruction: Open cdk/lib/config.ts. Find ALLOWED_IPS array (~line 55). Replace '0.0.0.0/32' with your IP followed by /32 (e.g. '203.0.113.45/32'). Save.
expected_result: ALLOWED_IPS contains your real IP with /32
notes: Add multiple IPs as separate array entries

command_or_instruction: Run: cd backend && mkdir -p layers/dependencies/python && pip install -r requirements.txt -t layers/dependencies/python/
expected_result: pypdf and python-docx appear in backend/layers/dependencies/python/
notes: boto3 is NOT included (already in Lambda runtime). Ignore pip version warnings.

command_or_instruction: Run: cd ../cdk && npm install
expected_result: node_modules created in cdk/ with no errors
notes: Installs TypeScript, CDK libraries

command_or_instruction: Run: cdk bootstrap
notes: One-time per account/region. Safe to run multiple times.

command_or_instruction: Run: cdk deploy RagToolStack --require-approval broadening -- Type 'y' when prompted for IAM changes
expected_result: Stack deploys. Outputs printed: DistributionUrl, DocumentsBucketName, FrontendBucketName, DistributionId, ApiUrl
notes: Takes 5-10 min. KB IDs not set yet (Lambdas won't work until step 24).

command_or_instruction: Copy all output values. Or run: aws cloudformation describe-stacks --stack-name RagToolStack --query Stacks[0].Outputs --output table
expected_result: You have: DistributionUrl, DocumentsBucketName, FrontendBucketName, DistributionId, ApiUrl saved
notes: You need these in later steps

command_or_instruction: Run 4 commands (replace BUCKET with DocumentsBucketName): aws s3api put-object --bucket BUCKET --key tech-approach/ --content-length 0 | aws s3api put-object --bucket BUCKET --key organizational-approach/ --content-length 0 | aws s3api put-object --bucket BUCKET --key past-performance/ --content-length 0 | aws s3api put-object --bucket BUCKET --key resumes/ --content-length 0
notes: Creates folder markers in S3

command_or_instruction: For each file: aws s3 cp filename.pdf s3://BUCKET/category-folder/ -- Example: aws s3 cp proposal.pdf s3://BUCKET/tech-approach/
expected_result: Files appear in S3. Verify: aws s3 ls s3://BUCKET/tech-approach/
notes: Upload to correct category folder. Bulk: aws s3 sync ./folder/ s3://BUCKET/category/

command_or_instruction: In AWS Console: Bedrock > Knowledge bases (left sidebar under Orchestration)
notes: This is where you create the RAG knowledge base

command_or_instruction: Click Create > 'Create a knowledge base with a vector store'
notes: Follow next 4 rows carefully

command_or_instruction: Name: rag-tool-kb | Description: RAG document search | IAM role: 'Create and use a new service role' | Click Next
notes: Bedrock creates an IAM role automatically

command_or_instruction: Name: rag-documents-s3 | Type: Amazon S3 | Click Browse S3 and select your documents bucket | Leave defaults | Click Next
notes: Points Bedrock at your S3 documents

command_or_instruction: Select 'Titan Text Embeddings V2' | Leave dimensions as 1024 | Click Next
notes: Converts text to vectors for search

command_or_instruction: Select 'Quick create a new vector store' | Choose 'Amazon S3 Vectors' | Leave defaults | Click Next
notes: S3 Vectors is cheapest (~$0.06/GB/month, scales to zero)

command_or_instruction: Review all settings. Click 'Create knowledge base'. Wait for status: Ready (1-3 min).
notes: If fails check IAM permissions

command_or_instruction: On KB detail page find the ID at top (e.g. ABCDEFGHIJ). Copy it.
expected_result: You have the Knowledge Base ID
notes: Needed for CDK redeploy

command_or_instruction: In Data sources section find the ID (e.g. XXXXXXXXXX). Copy it.
expected_result: You have the Data Source ID
notes: Needed for CDK redeploy and sync

command_or_instruction: Select your data source. Click Sync. Wait for green success banner.
notes: May take a few minutes. Or CLI: aws bedrock-agent start-ingestion-job --knowledge-base-id KB_ID --data-source-id DS_ID

command_or_instruction: Run: cd cdk && cdk deploy RagToolStack -c knowledgeBaseId=YOUR_KB_ID -c dataSourceId=YOUR_DS_ID --require-approval broadening
expected_result: Stack updates. Lambda env vars now include KNOWLEDGE_BASE_ID and DATA_SOURCE_ID
notes: This is critical - without this, search/upload Lambdas can't reach the KB

command_or_instruction: Run: cd ../frontend && npm install
notes: Installs React, TypeScript, Tailwind, Vite

command_or_instruction: Run: npm run build
expected_result: dist/ directory created with index.html and JS/CSS bundles
notes: Compiles and bundles the React app

command_or_instruction: Run: aws s3 sync dist/ s3://FRONTEND_BUCKET/ --delete (use FrontendBucketName from step 16)
notes: --delete removes old files

command_or_instruction: Run: aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id DIST_ID --paths '/*' (use DistributionId from step 16)
notes: Takes 1-2 min to propagate globally

command_or_instruction: Open DistributionUrl in browser (from allowed IP)
expected_result: RAG Document Tool loads with 3 tabs: Search & Generate, File Manager, Audit
notes: 403 = IP not in allowlist. Update config.ts and redeploy.

command_or_instruction: Click File Manager tab. Verify uploaded documents appear. Try uploading a new file.
expected_result: Documents listed by category. Upload succeeds.
notes: UI uploads auto-trigger KB sync

command_or_instruction: Click Search & Generate. Enter a query related to your documents. Select category and tone. Click Search.
expected_result: Results appear with filenames, passages, and scores
notes: No results? Check KB sync completed (step 28). Wait 2-3 min after sync.

command_or_instruction: Select search results with checkboxes. Click 'Proceed with Selected'. Wait 10-30 sec.
expected_result: Generated draft appears matching tone and reference style
notes: Check CloudWatch Logs for GenerateFunction if it fails

command_or_instruction: Click Audit tab. Upload a document + criteria file. Click Run Audit. Wait 1-2 min.
expected_result: Structured report: criteria, verdicts, evidence, recommendations
notes: Uses Claude Sonnet - slower but more thorough

command_or_instruction: Upload: aws s3 cp file.pdf s3://BUCKET/category/ | Then sync: either Bedrock console Sync button or: aws bedrock-agent start-ingestion-job --knowledge-base-id KB_ID --data-source-id DS_ID
notes: UI uploads auto-sync. CLI uploads need manual sync.

command_or_instruction: 1. Edit cdk/lib/config.ts CATEGORIES array | 2. cd cdk && cdk deploy RagToolStack -c knowledgeBaseId=X -c dataSourceId=Y | 3. Create S3 folder | 4. Upload docs + sync KB | 5. cd frontend && npm run build && aws s3 sync dist/ s3://FRONTEND_BUCKET/ --delete
notes: See README Adding New Categories section

command_or_instruction: 1. Edit ALLOWED_IPS in cdk/lib/config.ts | 2. cd cdk && cdk deploy RagToolStack -c knowledgeBaseId=X -c dataSourceId=Y
expected_result: New IPs allowed, removed IPs blocked
notes: Changes propagate within minutes
